name: Illinois Apple Pie
recipeInstructions:
Apple Filling:
6 cups Tart apples, peeled and thinly sliced
1/2 cup White sugar
1/2 cup Brown sugar
1 teaspoon Cinnamon
2 tablespoons Lemon juice
1/4 teaspoon Salt
1/4 teaspoon Nutmeg
2 tablespoons Butter
Crust Preparation:
Preheat oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit.
For the crust, combine flour and salt in a mixing bowl. Cut in shortening with a pastry blender or two knives until the mixture is uniform. Add water; toss lightly with a fork until dough forms a ball. Divide dough into two parts.
Rolling the Crust:
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the bottom crust to form a circle about 1/8 inch thick and approximately 1 1/2 inches larger than an inverted 9-inch pie plate. Gently ease the dough into the pie plate, being careful not to stretch the dough. Trim the edge even with the pie plate.
Assembling the Pie:
For the apple filling, combine all ingredients in a mixing bowl and stir until well mixed. Spoon the mixture into the unbaked pie shell.
Top Crust:
Use the remaining half of the dough to roll out the top crust, following the same instructions for the bottom crust. Lift it over the filled pie and trim 1/2 inch beyond the edge of the pie plate. Fold the top edge under the bottom crust and flute as desired. Cut slits or designs in the top crust to allow steam to escape. Brush the top crust with milk and sprinkle with white sugar.
Baking:
Bake at 450 degrees Fahrenheit for 20 minutes. Reduce heat to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and bake an additional 25 to 30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown. Cool and serve.
